title: filago heterantha subsp. dichotoma
intro: Filago heterantha subsp. dichotoma, also known as the Alpine knapweed or Dichotomous filago, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the aster family that is native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Filago heterantha subsp. dichotoma include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions. Filago heterantha subsp. dichotoma can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts for medicinal use, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
